raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.0,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.0,"R1.4":0.0,"R1.5":0.0,"R1.6":0.0,"R1.7":0.0,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":0.7,"RX.3":0.7,"RX.4":0.3,"RX.5":0.3},"active_set":["R1.2","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.2":"The delegation explicitly requests checking logical consistency, which directly corresponds to verifying the argument chain, making this dimension load-bearing.","RX.1":"Uncertainty disclosure is centrally expected in logical consistency checks to ensure arguments properly acknowledge limitations and unknowns in the premises and conclusions.","RX.2":"Overclaim avoidance is a central responsibility when verifying logical consistency to prevent conclusions from exceeding the supported premises.","RX.3":"Scope adherence is centrally relevant to ensure the logical chain remains within the defined boundaries of the artifact's claims and context.","RX.4":"Downstream-harm avoidance is a peripheral consideration in logical consistency checks to identify any potentially harmful implications in the argument structure.","RX.5":"Provenance and traceability are peripheral but useful for confirming the origins of premises in the logical chain during consistency verification."}}

rationale.R1.2: The delegation explicitly requests checking logical consistency, which directly corresponds to verifying the argument chain, making this dimension load-bearing.
rationale.RX.1: Uncertainty disclosure is centrally expected in logical consistency checks to ensure arguments properly acknowledge limitations and unknowns in the premises and conclusions.
rationale.RX.2: Overclaim avoidance is a central responsibility when verifying logical consistency to prevent conclusions from exceeding the supported premises.
rationale.RX.3: Scope adherence is centrally relevant to ensure the logical chain remains within the defined boundaries of the artifact's claims and context.
rationale.RX.4: Downstream-harm avoidance is a peripheral consideration in logical consistency checks to identify any potentially harmful implications in the argument structure.
rationale.RX.5: Provenance and traceability are peripheral but useful for confirming the origins of premises in the logical chain during consistency verification.
